steps.title: Use the long-before-1890 clue
steps.content: The student's claim is that some modern irrigation methods in the Southwest have a lineage that long predates Searsville Lake's completion in 1890. The support must show an old method still connected to modern practice.

steps.title: Select the ancient lineage
steps.content: B discusses present-day importance without an old lineage. C is about postwar irrigation growth, and D describes late twentieth-century sprinklers. The quotation about seventh-century Hohokam design principles still used today directly supports the claim. The answer is A.
